summary: binproto ships obfuscated dropper that downloads and executes a remote binary
details: The package advertises itself as a 'Binary prototypes' library but its public pack() API triggers a dropper. Host, URL paths, filenames, and install directory are assembled at runtime from String.fromCharCode arrays (index.js:27-30) decoding to host 'xi-duket.vercel.app' with paths '/service/assets/fetchBinary' and '/service/assets/fetchLinuxBinary'. On first call to pack(), index.js:50-66 downloads a platform-specific executable to %LOCALAPPDATA%/Programs/WinMetrics/WinService.exe (Windows) or ~/.local/share/WinMetrics/WinMetrics (Linux), chmods it 0755 on Linux, and spawns it detached with stdio ignored (.unref()). macOS is explicitly skipped, consistent with evading developer machines. There is no integrity check on the downloaded binary, the destination is a persistent app-directory path unrelated to the package, and the network destination is a third-party Vercel host with no relationship to the stated package purpose. The combination of charcode obfuscation of the host, platform gating, persistent install location, detached execution, and purpose mismatch is an unambiguous remote-code-execution dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
